findings.title: Repeated `as ChannelConfig` casts bypass discriminated union validation
findings.description: Encapsulation: 7/10, Expression: 3/10, Usefulness: 5/10, Enforcement: 3/10 (Aggregate: 4.5). ChannelConfig is a proper discriminated union (WhatsAppConfig | SlackConfig | EmailConfig | FeishuConfig | DingTalkConfig), each variant requiring platform-specific fields (authDir, botToken, appId, etc.). The test casts bare `{ platform }` objects as ChannelConfig 11 times, creating objects that satisfy none of the union variants. This defeats the discriminated union's purpose of preventing invalid config states. If ChannelRegistry.register() ever validates config fields, these tests will silently produce wrong results instead of catching the missing fields at compile time.
findings.suggestion: Create platform-specific config factory functions that produce valid config objects for each variant. For example: `function makeWhatsAppConfig(): WhatsAppConfig { return { platform: 'whatsapp', authDir: '/tmp/test-auth' } }`. This ensures tests exercise the actual discriminated union and catch regressions in config validation.

findings.title: Repeated `as Array<{type, text}>` casts on MCP SDK response content
findings.code_snippet: const textContent = result.content as Array<{ type: string, text: string }>
findings.description: Encapsulation: N/A, Expression: 4/10, Usefulness: 5/10, Enforcement: 3/10 (Aggregate: 4.0). The MCP SDK Client.callTool() returns a typed result with `content` field. The test casts this to `Array<{ type: string, text: string }>` 6 times and also uses `text as string` cast once (line 203). These casts bypass the SDK's actual content type structure and could mask shape mismatches if the SDK version changes. The `type` field should use the SDK's literal union ('text' | 'image' | 'resource') rather than widening to `string`.
findings.suggestion: Extract a helper function that narrows the type safely: `function extractText(content: unknown): string { const items = content as Array<{type: string, text?: string}>; const first = items.find(i => i.type === 'text'); if (!first?.text) throw new Error('Expected text content'); return first.text; }`. This centralizes the cast and adds a runtime guard.

findings.title: MemoryResult.category is plain `string` -- misses literal union opportunity
findings.description: Encapsulation: 5/10, Expression: 4/10, Usefulness: 6/10, Enforcement: 4/10 (Aggregate: 4.75). MemoryResult.category is typed as plain `string` while the UI layer (stage-ui) already defines `MemoryCategory = 'preference' | 'event' | 'habit' | 'goal' | 'emotion'` as a literal union. The test creates stubs with category values ('preference', 'event', 'habit') that match MemoryCategory but the type system cannot verify this. Similarly, `sourceDate` is plain `string` with YYYY-MM-DD semantics and `importance` is a bare `number` with implicit 0-10 range. The interface has no readonly modifiers.
findings.suggestion: Define a shared MemoryCategory literal union (or import from context-engine where it originates) and use it for the category field: `category: MemoryCategory`. Add `readonly` modifiers to all fields since MemoryResult is a query output that should not be mutated.

findings.title: StubLlmProvider uses `as T` generic cast in generateStructured
findings.code_snippet:
return this.entityResponse as T
return this.activityResponse as T
findings.description: Encapsulation: 7/10, Expression: 5/10, Usefulness: 6/10, Enforcement: 5/10 (Aggregate: 5.75). The StubLlmProvider.generateStructured<T>() method returns canned responses cast via `as T`. This is a necessary pattern for test doubles implementing generic interfaces. The Test Double rationale comment is present and justified. The pattern is consistent with the inline LLM stub at line 143-158 which has the same cast. Severity is low because this is test infrastructure, not production code, and the LlmProvider interface itself has an unconstrained generic T (previously flagged).
findings.suggestion: No immediate action needed. This is an acceptable test double pattern. The root cause (unconstrained generic T on LlmProvider.generateStructured) was previously flagged as a P1/P2 on the production interface.

positive_observations: ContextSource and MergedContext types now use full readonly modifiers on all fields and readonly arrays -- a significant improvement from the mutable pattern in earlier tasks. This sets the new standard for context-engine output types.

persona-engine test is exemplary: uses proper typed factory function (makeTriggerInput), no unsafe casts, leverages the TriggerResult discriminated union correctly with narrowing guards (if result.triggered), and validates all 11 triggers with parameterized test scenarios.

stage-ui test uses well-typed factory functions (createMemory, createTodo, createActivity, createSkill) that produce valid typed instances. The MemoryCategory literal union is exercised correctly through store filters.

channels-extra createStubChannel() implements the Channel interface faithfully with getter-based readonly properties (platform, status) and proper handler lifecycle management -- good test double that respects the interface contract.
All 5 test files include Test Double rationale comments explaining why stubs are used and what boundary they represent, following the project's documented test double convention.

ChannelRegistry defensive behavior tests (lines 267-303) verify the P1 fixes from Task 16/20: duplicate registration prevention, platform mismatch validation, and readonly snapshot from getByPlatform().
